title: Schizopetalon viride
taxonomy: Schizopetalon viride is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. This family, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family, consists of over 400 genera and includes a wide range of plants such as broccoli, cauliflower, and mustard. Schizopetalon viride is further classified as a member of the genus Schizopetalon, which contains a few species of flowering plants native to the western United States and southwestern Canada. The genus Schizopetalon is part of the tribe Lepidieae within the Brassicaceae family. Schizopetalon viride is a rare and unique species that is known for its distinctive flowers and growth habits.
